candidate_problems.text: How can 'survives criticism' be operationalized for token-predicting systems without smuggling in human judgment or architecture-based assumptions? The conjecture shifts the criterion from mechanism to explanatory performance, but it leaves open what concrete tests distinguish compressive, counterfactual, error-correctable explanation from fluent post hoc rationalization.

What makes an output count as genuinely new knowledge rather than a recombination of training-derived fragments, if all knowledge growth is transformative rearrangement? The conjecture rejects the simple novelty/repetition boundary, but it still needs a principled account of when problem-solving success reflects discovery of explanatory structure rather than accidental synthesis or benchmark overfitting.

If the decisive test is historical and epistemic rather than architectural, what social or institutional process should certify machine-generated knowledge claims? The conjecture implies that knowledge status depends on criticism, variation, and continued problem-solving, raising the unresolved question of whether current scientific norms can absorb AI systems as participants in knowledge creation or whether new evaluative institutions are needed.
